prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: Verily, in the third quarter of this most bewildering year, the company's revenue didst increase by 27%, a sum most fantastical and beyond the reckoning of mortal men. 'Tis said that the sales of their "cloud-based subscription model" didst soar like a flock of starlings on wing, leaving in its wake a trail of gold and silver that didst gladden the hearts of the company's noble investors. Alas, the "customer acquisition costs" didst rise like a mighty dragon, devouring a full 30% of the company's treasure, leaving some to wonder if the very fabric of their business model was not rent asunder. Meanwhile, the "churn rate" didst remain steady, a constant and mysterious force that didst claim 5% of the company's customers each month, like a dark and malevolent tide that none couldst fully comprehend.
